h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’d always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that does make it feel more like a rule than a mistake.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was initially treating their dialect usage as careless errors to be identified and corrected for academic writing.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply apply the previously established operational logic to the new 0.4-second metric and calculate the capacity.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think my first instinct was still picturing one big flood doing the main work, because the canyon is so large.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The exchange was heading toward a continued negotiation of the mathematical scale between a single flood and a million years of erosion.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I’m still stuck on the word “innocent,” though, because it sounds like a verdict before the verdict.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think that fixes the note
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to treat the word 'innocent' as a confusing vocabulary term requiring a simpler synonym or further tutor explanation.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will simply append a beyond-plant effect to their existing explanation as instructed by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Maybe the better way to say it is: the salt is gone as little grains I can see, but not gone from the sealed cup.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply answer the tutor's question about what would be left behind on the dish after the water dries.
